key_insight: A unified DVAE + particle filter framework can monitor arc welding states robustly by learning compact latent dynamics from weld pool images while filtering out disturbances in real time.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable, process-general monitoring for welding lines such as GTAW and GMAW without heavy labeled-data dependence or process-specific tuning.
method: CNN-based encoder-decoder learns latent weld-pool representations from images
LSTM transition model captures time-evolving process dynamics under control inputs
Particle filter tracks latent and hidden states to suppress noise and preserve history
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on GTAW and GMAW and emphasizes generalization and robustness without process-specific tuning.
watch_item: Performance may depend on stable image quality and careful particle-filter design, especially under severe arc radiation and specular reflection.
paper_title: Robust Monitoring of Arc Welding Processes: A Generalizable Framework with DVAE and Particle Filter
